key_insights.insight: New beginnings often emerge from dark or challenging circumstances, just as a new day starts in darkness at midnight.
key_insights.quote: So if a new day and a new beginning starts in the dark every day, that's when your new beginnings start.

key_insights.insight: Winning involves understanding and managing your 'dark side' or negative traits, not running from them.
key_insights.quote: You have to visit that place because if that place comes visit you, it will never leave.

key_insights.insight: Success brings its own unique mental health challenges due to increased pressure and constant scrutiny.
key_insights.quote: That's a whole different level of mental health that success brings that a lot of people just don't understand.

key_insights.insight: Obsession, not mere interest, is required to achieve exceptional results and impact the world.
key_insights.quote: Interested people watch obsessed people change the world.

key_insights.insight: Obsession requires attention to detail that others neglect, controlling every manageable aspect to better handle the uncontrollable.
key_insights.quote: These individuals, they want to control everything they can control so the uncontrollable becomes more manageable to them.

key_insights.insight: True balance isn't found but actively created by eliminating inessentials and surrounding yourself with people that support your priorities.
key_insights.quote: You don't find balance, you create it...Get closer to balance by deleting the unessentials.

key_insights.insight: Accountability must be maintained to stay at the top, and honesty with yourself and others is critical.
key_insights.quote: Staying on the top is not the same thing as reaching the top. Many individuals can reach the top, but very few individuals stay at the top because the accountability among their team and among themselves starts to deteriorate once they've reached the top.

key_insights.insight: Winning might cost you everything, but the rewards will be so much more.
key_insights.quote: Winning will cost you everything, but will reward you with so much more.

key_insights.insight: If you think the price of winning is too high, wait till you get the bill from regret.
key_insights.quote: If you think the price of winning is too high, wait till you get the bill from regret.

actionable_advice.advice: Visit your 'dark side' or confront past traumas intentionally.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set aside time for introspection, journaling, or therapy to process negative experiences and understand their impact.

actionable_advice.advice: Focus on maximizing your focus, not just your time.
actionable_advice.how_to: Eliminate distractions, prioritize essential tasks, and create a dedicated environment for deep work.
Audit your activities and commitments, identifying what's not contributing to your goals and eliminating them ruthlessly.

actionable_advice.advice: Build your brand by focusing on outperforming others and creating a superior product.
actionable_advice.how_to: Concentrate on doing your job better than anyone else, focusing on quality and exceeding expectations.

actionable_advice.advice: When making decisions, choose your mind over your feelings.
actionable_advice.how_to: Create a structured decision process, and when faced with that process, try to disassociate yourself from your feeling/emotions towards that decision.

memorable_stories: Tim Grover's father, a former professor, worked dismantling cadavers to provide for his family.
Michael Jordan walking to the free throw line before the game and stating that the basket was off. Maintenence came to measure and it was off by 1/8th of an inch.
Grover's daughter's emotional offer to 'eat less' if it meant he would travel less, highlighting the personal sacrifices involved in his demanding career.
Grover calculating Michael Jordan's steps for each leg in the old days to make sure each muscle group was worked efficiently.
contrarian_claims: Winning doesn't make you heartless, but it teaches you to use your heart less.
Showing up is none of the battle.
The most driven individuals they live a life for many years and certain times without balance.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Man Who Coached Michael Jordan AND Kobe Bryant To WIN!: Tim Grover
